About Me

I'm Kristian López, a Software Architect and Staff Engineer with 7+ years of experience designing distributed systems that survive contact with reality. My work sits at the intersection of clean architecture, cloud-native deployment, and developer experience.

My engineering philosophy is simple: complexity is the enemy. I design for replaceability — systems where any component can be swapped, tested, and reasoned about independently. I rely on Hexagonal Architecture, DDD, and Event-Driven patterns not because they're fashionable, but because they work.

Outside of architecture decisions, I enjoy writing about the trade-offs I've made in production, mentoring engineers through the jump from senior to staff, and exploring what Rust can do in domains traditionally owned by JVM languages.

Specialties

Hexagonal ArchitectureClean ArchitectureDomain-Driven DesignCQRSEvent-Driven ArchitectureMicroservicesEvent SourcingReactive Systems

Architecture Principles

  • • Ports & Adapters (Hexagonal) as the default shape
  • • Bounded contexts over shared databases
  • • Event-first design — facts, not commands
  • • Testability as a first-class design constraint
  • • Evolutionary architecture over big-bang rewrites

Cloud & Infrastructure

  • • AWS: EKS, S3, SQS, SNS, Secrets Manager, IAM
  • • Kubernetes: workloads, RBAC, Helm, GitOps
  • • Observability: OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, Grafana
  • • CI/CD: GitHub Actions, ArgoCD
  • • IaC: Terraform, CDK
